prompt_text:
### Demographics: 42-year-old Asian man

### Visit type: Follow-up visit (12-month follow-up)

### Reason for visit / current symptoms
Reason for visit: Routine follow-up (every 3 months) | Diagnosis context: 6 years ago Diagnosed with type 2 diabetes during urgent care evaluation for fatigue/blurred vision
Current symptoms: No DKA/HHS symptoms; no recent hypoglycemia episodes; no acute complaints; Despite repeated counseling on insulin need, the patient strongly refuses injections; shared decision was made to intensify oral therapy and reassess within 2-4 weeks.

### History
- Other conditions: Chronic kidney disease Stage 3a (45-59)
ASCVD: Ischemic stroke or transient ischemic attack history
Simple Steatosis
Body mass index category: Normal
Diabetic neuropathy
Diabetic Peripheral Neuropathy
Recurrent Complicated Urinary Tract Infections
- Diabetes-related comorbidities: CKD: Stage 3a (45-59); HF (No); ASCVD: Ischemic stroke or transient ischemic attack history; MASLD: Simple Steatosis; BMI Category: Normal
- Family history: No family history of type 2 diabetes (elicited)
- Social history: Never smoker; Alcohol 5-6 drinks/week (1-2 per sitting)

### Medication information
- Current medications: Metformin 1000mg twice daily; Glimepiride 4mg once daily; Semaglutide 1mg once weekly; Rosuvastatin (10 mg daily), Lisinopril (10 mg daily), Finerenone (10 mg daily), Ciprofloxacin (500 mg twice daily for 7 days)

### Physical exam (current)
Weight 61.7 kg; Height 173.9 cm; BMI 20.4 kg/m²; Blood pressure 146/81 mmHg

### Laboratory values (with date / relative timing)
Format: YYYY-MM-DD value or relative timing (12 months ago / 9 months ago / 6 months ago / 3 months ago / current)
Order: 12 months ago -> 9 months ago -> 6 months ago -> 3 months ago -> current
- Glycemia: HbA1c (12 months ago 8.0%; 9 months ago 7.7%; 6 months ago 7.5%; 3 months ago 7.6%; current 10.0%), FPG (12 months ago 170mg/dL; 9 months ago 157mg/dL; 6 months ago 157mg/dL; 3 months ago 204mg/dL; current 300mg/dL)
- Anthropometrics / blood pressure: Weight (12 months ago 60.3kg; 9 months ago 61.9kg; 6 months ago 60.2kg; 3 months ago 60.1kg; current 61.7kg), Height (12 months ago 173.9cm; 9 months ago 173.9cm; 6 months ago 173.9cm; 3 months ago 173.9cm; current 173.9cm), BMI (12 months ago 20.0kg/m²; 9 months ago 20.0kg/m²; 6 months ago 20.0kg/m²; 3 months ago 20.0kg/m²; current 20.4kg/m²), SBP (12 months ago 147mmHg; 9 months ago 143mmHg; 6 months ago 142mmHg; 3 months ago 144mmHg; current 146mmHg), DBP (12 months ago 82mmHg; 9 months ago 80mmHg; 6 months ago 84mmHg; 3 months ago 83mmHg; current 81mmHg)
- Liver function: ALT (12 months ago 50U/L; 9 months ago 44U/L; 6 months ago 50U/L; 3 months ago 43U/L; current 48U/L), AST (12 months ago 46U/L; 9 months ago 52U/L; 6 months ago 53U/L; 3 months ago 52U/L; current 51U/L), Total Bilirubin (12 months ago 0.6mg/dL; 9 months ago 0.6mg/dL; 6 months ago 0.6mg/dL; 3 months ago 0.6mg/dL; current 0.6mg/dL)
- Kidney: eGFR (12 months ago 54mL/min/1.73m²; 9 months ago 52mL/min/1.73m²; 6 months ago 52mL/min/1.73m²; 3 months ago 48mL/min/1.73m²; current 49mL/min/1.73m²), UACR (12 months ago 185mg/g; 9 months ago 208mg/g; 6 months ago 212mg/g; 3 months ago 215mg/g; current 269mg/g)
- Lipids: LDL (12 months ago 69mg/dL; 9 months ago 69mg/dL; 6 months ago 69mg/dL; 3 months ago 74mg/dL; current 71mg/dL); HDL (12 months ago 35mg/dL; 9 months ago 38mg/dL; 6 months ago 34mg/dL; 3 months ago 35mg/dL; current 36mg/dL); Triglycerides (12 months ago 151mg/dL; 9 months ago 150mg/dL; 6 months ago 150mg/dL; 3 months ago 154mg/dL; current 159mg/dL)
- Hemoglobin: Hemoglobin (12 months ago 13.0g/dL; 9 months ago 13.8g/dL; 6 months ago 13.1g/dL; 3 months ago 13.0g/dL; current 12.9g/dL)
- Additional tests: urine ketone Not measured (Current); fasting C-peptide 0.88 ng/mL (Current)

### Individualized treatment decision factors
Current adherence and self-management: Very poor adherence (estimated <50%); SMBG rarely performed; lifestyle control severely limited. Strong injection refusal is documented; under shared decision-making, the patient accepts oral regimen adjustment with short-interval reassessment.
Financial context: Patient has comprehensive insurance with minimal out-of-pocket burden and reports no financial barriers to clinically indicated therapies (budget ≤ KRW 1,000,000/month).
